items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2014-9222 surged from 12% to 87% (percentile 100%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2014-era vulnerability. items.description: SimpleHelp contains an authentication bypass vulnerability in the OIDC authentication flow. priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). Known ransomware campaign use. EPSS 70% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.3.

w5h.what_impacted: Systems running Check Point Security Gateway
w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for Check Point Security Gateway per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-06-11)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 70% within 30 days; critical severity means full compromise is likely if reached; edge/network devices are internet-facing and a top initial-access vector.

impact.affected_versions: Check Point Security Gateway (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Identify all internet-facing Check Point Security Gateway devices. Check firmware versions against the advisory, and review access logs for unauthorized access, unusual processes, or configuration changes.

items.title: BerriAI LiteLLM Command Injection Vulnerability
items.description: BerriAI LiteLLM contains a command injection vulnerability that could allow any authenticated user, including holders of low-privilege internal-user keys, to run arbitrary commands on the host.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 80% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 8.8.

w5h.upgrade_action: Apply current vendor patches for BerriAI LiteLLM per the advisory (CISA remediation due date: 2026-06-22)
w5h.why_it_matters: This matters because exploitation is confirmed or actively occurring, not theoretical; EPSS puts exploitation probability at 80% within 30 days.

impact.affected_versions: BerriAI LiteLLM (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all BerriAI LiteLLM installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.
